commands.name: completion
commands.long:
Generate shell completion script for kmux.

To install completions:

Bash:
  $ kmux completion bash > /etc/bash_completion.d/kmux

Zsh:
  $ kmux completion zsh > "${fpath[1]}/_kmux"

Fish:
  $ kmux completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/kmux.fish

commands.name: config
commands.long:
View and modify kmux configuration settings.

Available subcommands:
- get: View configuration values
- set: Update configuration values
- path: Show configuration file path

commands.name: doctor
commands.long:
Run diagnostics on your kmux installation and configuration.

Checks:
- tmux installation and version
- Config file validity
- Project registry integrity
- Port range allocations
- State file consistency

commands.name: down
commands.short: Stop a kmux project (kill tmux session)
commands.long:
Stop a kmux project by killing its tmux session.

If no project name is provided, uses the current directory (if registered).
If a project name is provided, stops that project regardless of current directory.

This command kills the tmux session and transitions the project from RUNNING or SUSPENDED
to STOPPED state. Port allocations remain registered but unused.

commands.name: init
commands.short: Register current directory as a kmux project
commands.long:
Register the current directory as a kmux project.

This command creates a .kmux.yaml configuration file in the current directory
and registers the project in the global kmux registry with an allocated port range.

The project name is used as a unique identifier across all kmux projects.
If --name is not provided, you will be prompted to enter a name (the directory
name is offered as a default).

Use --force to overwrite an existing .kmux.yaml file or to re-register a project
that was previously registered at the same path.

flags.name: port
flags.usage: custom port range start (default: auto-assign next available)

commands.name: list
commands.long:
List all registered kmux projects with their state, port range, and path.

Output is displayed as a table showing:
- Project name
- State (STOPPED, RUNNING, SUSPENDED)
- Port range (e.g., 3000-3099)
- Absolute path to project directory

The active project (if any) is highlighted.

commands.name: port
commands.short: Show allocated port range for a project
commands.long:
Show the allocated port range for a kmux project.

If no project name is provided, shows port range for the current directory (if registered).
If a project name is provided, shows port range for that project.

Each project gets a dedicated 100-port range (e.g., 3000-3099, 3100-3199).
The PORT environment variable is set to the range start for all tmux panes.

commands.name: setup
commands.long:
Run or re-run the kmux setup wizard.

The wizard lets you configure analytics, shell completion, tmux verification,
and default port ranges.

Use --reset to replay the retro animation (like first run).

flags.name: reset
flags.usage: show the retro animation (like first run)

commands.name: status
commands.short: Show project status and tmux window details
commands.long:
Show the status of a kmux project including its state, port range, and tmux window details.

If no project name is provided, shows status for the current directory (if registered).
If a project name is provided, shows status for that project.

Displays:
- Project state (STOPPED, RUNNING, SUSPENDED)
- Port range allocation
- tmux session name and window list
- Pane details for each window

commands.name: switch
commands.short: Switch to a different kmux project
commands.long:
Switch your entire development context to a different project.

This command:
- Suspends the currently active project (if any)
- Resumes the target project (or starts it if stopped)
- Switches tmux client to the target project's session

One command switches your entire context: tmux session, port range, environment, and AI tool awareness.

commands.name: up
commands.short: Start a kmux project (tmux session + ports)
commands.long:
Start a kmux project by launching its tmux session and allocating its port range.

If no project name is provided, the current directory is used (if registered as a project).
If a project name is provided, kmux will cd to that project's directory and start it.

This command:
- Creates a tmux session with windows/panes as defined in .kmux.yaml
- Sets PORT and PORT_RANGE environment variables for all panes
- Runs any startup commands defined in the config
- Transitions the project from STOPPED to RUNNING state

flags.name: attach
flags.usage: attach to the tmux session after starting

commands.name: version
commands.long: Display the version number of kmux.
